passage.title: MGT Set #9001
passage.text: The school library wanted younger students to find books more easily. At first, the student helpers made a map with many colors, but the labels were very small. Several first graders said the map looked pretty yet took too long to read. After hearing that comment, the helpers grouped the shelves by simple symbols and added larger arrows. They also placed short signs at each corner so students could check their direction again when they forgot the first turn. On the next test day, most students reached the animal and science shelves without extra help. The team noticed that clearer signs worked better than decorative details. Because of that result, the library decided to update the rest of the room in the same way.

questions.prompt: What is the passage mainly about?
choices: Improving library signs so younger students can find books
Painting the library with many bright colors
Asking students to read more science books
Moving the animal shelf to a new room
questions.rationale: The passage focuses on how the library team improved the map and signs.

questions.prompt: What problem did students mention about the first map?
choices: The labels were too small to read quickly
The map had no colors at all
The arrows pointed outside the library
The shelves were missing from the map
questions.rationale: Students said the map looked pretty but took too long to read because the labels were small.

questions.prompt: What did the helpers place at each corner?
choices: Short signs
Bean plants
Reading lamps
Extra maps
questions.rationale: The passage says they placed short signs at each corner.

questions.prompt: What does decorative details mean in the seventh sentence?
choices: Pretty parts that are not the most useful
Books about art history
The names of shelf groups
Special rules for helpers
questions.rationale: Decorative details are the pretty-looking parts that did not help students as much.

questions.prompt: Why did clearer signs work better for younger students?
choices: They could understand directions faster
They made the room look larger
They taught students to draw maps
They reduced the number of books
questions.rationale: Younger students needed quick, clear directions, so simpler signs helped them more.

questions.prompt: What will the library most likely do next?
choices: Change more areas to use the clearer sign style
Remove all maps from the walls
Ask first graders to build new shelves
Use smaller labels on every corner
questions.rationale: The last sentence says the library decided to update the rest of the room in the same way.
